subject: ap-world-history
title: Imperialism & Colonialism
stimulus:
“Industrialization reduced [tea production] costs dramatically. In 1872 the production cost of a
pound of tea was roughly the same in India and China. [But] by 1913 the cost of production in
India had fallen by three-quarters.... In the space of a few years China had been dethroned [by
India] as Britain’s main supplier of tea.
The figures tell the story: Britain imported thirty-one thousand tons of tea from China in 1859,
but by 1899 that total had fallen to seven thousand tons, while imports from India had risen to
nearly one hundred thousand tons. The rise of India’s tea industry had a devastating impact on
China’s tea farmers and further contributed to the instability of the country, which descended
into a chaotic period of rebellions, revolutions, and wars.”
Source: Tom Standage, British journalist, A History of the World in 6 Glasses, book published in
2006

parts.prompt:
Identify one argument the author makes regarding tea production in the late nineteenth and
early twentieth centuries.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.

required_elements: The cost of production in India fell compared to the cost in China.
alternatives.correct_example: The cost of production in India fell compared to the cost in China.

required_elements: China was “dethroned” by India as the primary supplier of tea for England.
alternatives.correct_example: China was “dethroned” by India as the primary supplier of tea for England.

required_elements: The Chinese tea farmers were devastated due to the rise of the Indian tea industry.
alternatives.correct_example: The Chinese tea farmers were devastated due to the rise of the Indian tea industry.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.

parts.prompt: Describe one likely reason for the change in tea imports to Britain, as outlined by the author.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.

required_elements: Britain’s colonization of India allowed it to expand tea production through plantations.
alternatives.correct_example: Britain’s colonization of India allowed it to expand tea production through plantations.

required_elements: One likely reason for the change in tea imports to Britain was because production costs in India were significantly lower than in China, making the Indian tea industry much more appealing to the British.
alternatives.correct_example: One likely reason for the change in tea imports to Britain was because production costs in India were significantly lower than in China, making the Indian tea industry much more appealing to the British.

required_elements: Transportation costs from India to Britain were likely lower than transportation costs from China to Britain because of the shorter geographical distance.
alternatives.correct_example: Transportation costs from India to Britain were likely lower than transportation costs from China to Britain because of the shorter geographical distance.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.

parts.prompt:
Explain how one additional piece of evidence, not included in the passage, would support
the author’s claim that China “descended into a chaotic period” in the nineteenth and early
twentieth centuries.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.

required_elements: The 1900–1901 Boxer Rebellion against foreigners and the subsequent foreign intervention in China resulted in longer-term disorder and significant loss of life.
alternatives.correct_example: The 1900–1901 Boxer Rebellion against foreigners and the subsequent foreign intervention in China resulted in longer-term disorder and significant loss of life.

required_elements: The Opium Wars launched China’s “Century of Humiliation”, forcing China to sign a series of unequal treaties which destabilized the imperial dynasty.
alternatives.correct_example: The Opium Wars launched China’s “Century of Humiliation”, forcing China to sign a series of unequal treaties which destabilized the imperial dynasty.

required_elements: The Revolution of 1911 forcibly ended the rule of the Qing Dynasty and started a prolonged period of political fragmentation, warlordism, and military conflicts within China.
alternatives.correct_example: The Revolution of 1911 forcibly ended the rule of the Qing Dynasty and started a prolonged period of political fragmentation, warlordism, and military conflicts within China.

required_elements: The Chinese Civil War between the KMT and CCP was a bitter, decades-long conflict, exacerbated by Japanese intervention, which eventually became a full-scale Japanese invasion of China.
alternatives.correct_example: The Chinese Civil War between the KMT and CCP was a bitter, decades-long conflict, exacerbated by Japanese intervention, which eventually became a full-scale Japanese invasion of China.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.
